news_summary: Northern Ireland has introduced miscarriage leave, the World Cup highlights sport's role in promoting unity, and benefits and pensions increase with the end of the two-child cap.
news_summary_fr: L'Irlande du Nord a instauré un congé en cas de fausse couche, la Coupe du monde met en évidence le rôle du sport dans la promotion de l'unité, et les prestations sociales et les retraites augmentent avec la suppression du plafond de deux enfants.
news_summary_es: Irlanda del Norte ha introducido un permiso por aborto espontáneo, el Mundial pone de relieve el papel del deporte en la promoción de la unidad, y las prestaciones y las pensiones aumentan tras la supresión del límite de dos hijos.

articles.title: 'Two weeks will make such a difference': UK first as Northern Ireland brings in miscarriage leave
articles.summary: Northern Ireland becomes first part of UK to bring in legal entitlement for parents affected by miscarriage at any stage of a pregnancy to have paid leave.
articles.content: But now, for her and others in Northern Ireland, there is an answer in extra support as it has become the first part of the UK where a woman and her partner are entitled to two weeks of paid leave if they experience a miscarriage at any stage of pregnancy.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant legal advancement in Northern Ireland, granting paid miscarriage leave to parents, which provides tangible emotional and financial support during a difficult time. This policy change has broad societal impact by recognizing and addressing the needs of grieving parents, setting a precedent for human rights and workplace support.
articles.personality_title: Northern Ireland leads UK with new paid miscarriage leave law
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Miscarriage is the loss of a pregnancy, which can be very difficult emotionally and financially for parents. Until now, there was no legal right in the UK for parents to take paid time off work after a miscarriage.

**What happened** – Northern Ireland has become the first part of the UK to introduce a law giving parents two weeks of paid leave if they have a miscarriage at any stage of pregnancy. This means both the mother and her partner can take time off work with pay to recover and support each other.

**Impact** – This new law offers important help by recognizing the emotional pain parents face and giving them time to heal without worrying about losing income. It also sets a new example for workplaces and governments to better support families during tough times.

**What's next step** – Other parts of the UK may consider similar laws after seeing how this one works in Northern Ireland. Employers will need to adjust their policies to follow the new rules and support their staff.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Northern Ireland’s new paid miscarriage leave law provides crucial support to grieving parents and could inspire wider change across the UK.
articles.personality_title_fr: L’Irlande du Nord pionnière au Royaume-Uni avec un congé payé après une fausse couche
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Une fausse couche est la perte d’une grossesse, ce qui peut être très difficile sur le plan émotionnel et financier pour les parents. Jusqu’à présent, il n’y avait pas de droit légal au Royaume-Uni pour prendre un congé payé après une fausse couche.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – L’Irlande du Nord est devenue la première région du Royaume-Uni à adopter une loi accordant aux parents deux semaines de congé payé en cas de fausse couche à n’importe quel stade de la grossesse. Cela signifie que la mère et son partenaire peuvent prendre un congé payé pour se remettre et se soutenir.

**Impact** – Cette nouvelle loi apporte une aide importante en reconnaissant la douleur émotionnelle des parents et en leur donnant le temps de guérir sans craindre une perte de revenu. Elle donne aussi un exemple pour les lieux de travail et les gouvernements afin de mieux soutenir les familles en période difficile.

**Prochaine étape** – D’autres régions du Royaume-Uni pourraient envisager des lois similaires après avoir vu comment celle-ci fonctionne en Irlande du Nord. Les employeurs devront adapter leurs politiques pour respecter ces nouvelles règles et soutenir leurs employés.

**Résumé en une phrase** – La nouvelle loi nord-irlandaise sur le congé payé après une fausse couche offre un soutien essentiel aux parents en deuil et pourrait inspirer un changement plus large au Royaume-Uni.
articles.personality_title_es: Irlanda del Norte lidera en Reino Unido con nuevo permiso pagado tras un aborto espontáneo
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Un aborto espontáneo es la pérdida de un embarazo, lo que puede ser muy difícil emocional y económicamente para los padres. Hasta ahora, no existía un derecho legal en el Reino Unido para tomar permiso pagado tras un aborto espontáneo.

**Qué pasó** – Irlanda del Norte se convirtió en la primera región del Reino Unido en aprobar una ley que da a los padres dos semanas de permiso pagado si sufren un aborto espontáneo en cualquier etapa del embarazo. Esto significa que la madre y su pareja pueden ausentarse del trabajo con pago para recuperarse y apoyarse mutuamente.

**Impacto** – Esta nueva ley ofrece ayuda importante al reconocer el dolor emocional de los padres y darles tiempo para sanar sin preocuparse por perder ingresos. También marca un ejemplo para los lugares de trabajo y gobiernos para apoyar mejor a las familias en momentos difíciles.

**Próximo paso** – Otras partes del Reino Unido podrían considerar leyes similares tras ver cómo funciona esta en Irlanda del Norte. Los empleadores deberán ajustar sus políticas para cumplir las nuevas reglas y apoyar a sus empleados.

**Resumen en una frase** – La nueva ley de Irlanda del Norte sobre permiso pagado tras un aborto espontáneo brinda apoyo crucial a padres en duelo y podría inspirar un cambio más amplio en el Reino Unido.
articles.image_prompt: A gentle, warm-toned painting of two intertwined hands holding a small, delicate sprouting seedling together, set against a soft, simple background symbolizing Northern Ireland’s landscape, conveying support, healing, and new beginnings after loss.

articles.title: As World Cup kick-off nears, a reminder of the power of sport to build bridges and break barriers
articles.summary: The 2026 World Cup final will take place at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, a few miles away from UN Headquarters where, on Wednesday, elite athletes and sports administrators spoke about the power of football and other international sports to change the world for the better.
articles.content:
Elite footballers will make the headlines this summer, but sport can have a transformative effect in every society, connecting communities across borders and generations, and creating spaces for dialogue, solidarity, and mutual respect.

In just over two months, the world’s biggest sporting tournament will get underway in Mexico City and, as usual, billions of people worldwide are expected to tune in.

From 11 June to 19 July, the World Cup will pit 48 national football (or, if you’re based in the US, “soccer”) teams against each other over 104 games to be played out in Mexico, Canada and the US.

© UNICEF/Gabriel Mmina/Elephant

In each of the host city regions, across the three countries, a programme called Play Collective will provide funding and support for community sport organisations, creating safe spaces for underserved youth.

The project is a joint initiative of the Adidas Foundation, the Beyond Sport foundation and Common Goal, a global non-profit which has built a worldwide network of community-based organizations that serves 3.6 million youth each year.

Make space for one another

United Nations Mary Connor (2nd left) and Nawal El Moutawakal speaking at UN Headquarters

Common Goal’s Executive Director, Mary Connor, was one of the key speakers at Building Bridges, Breaking Barriers, an event held to mark the International Day of Sport for Development and Peace, which takes place each year on 6 April to highlight the transformative power of sport in driving social change, advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), and bringing together people and communities from around the world.

Ms. Connor drew parallels between football, where supporting teammates is central to success, and the work of the United Nations, a place where, she said, “we come together across differences, across cultures that keep us isolated” and “make space for one another to find a way through, against a lot of headwinds.”

Nawal El Moutawakel, pioneering Olympian

In two years, the United States is set to play host to another huge sporting jamboree, the 2028 Summer Olympics, which will be based in Los Angeles.

Pioneering athlete Nawal El Moutawakel was one of the stars of the last Olympics to be held in LA. At the 1984 Games, she became the first Moroccan, African, Arab, and Muslim woman to win an Olympic gold medal (for the 400 metre hurdles).

Her Olympic triumph is seen as a turning point, giving Moroccan women confidence and the courage to pursue sports, which had previously been considered a male domain.

She has received numerous international honours for her contributions to sport and social progress and is currently Vice President of the International Olympic Committee.

© UNICEF/Tsiory Andriantsoarana

Jumping the hurdles of life

Speaking at Wednesday’s event, Ms. El Moutawakel likened her discipline to the struggles she faced in her early sporting career.

“My race was the 400 hurdles, a race where there is a start and a finish, and in between there are 10 hurdles. And for me, they were the hurdles of life that teach you discipline, coordination, determination, passion. Sometimes you experience failures, but I never gave up.”

Today, she said, the fact that, for the first time in 100 years, the IOC President is a woman (Kirsty Coventry is also the first African to hold the office), and that women make up 50 per cent of the organisation at all levels, points to the progress that is being made.

Ms. El Moutawakel and Ms. Connor were joined by several prominent youth athletes, who described the positive influence that sport has had on their lives.

They included Ailyn Lopez, who overcame mental health issues through football, and is now a young leader with Street Child United, an organisation that uses sport to support young people living on the streets or in extreme poverty.
articles.source: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
articles.reasoning: The article highlights the transformative power of sport to build bridges, promote social inclusion, and advance gender equality and youth empowerment on a global scale. It focuses on concrete initiatives like the Play Collective supporting underserved youth, the historic achievements of Nawal El Moutawakel breaking barriers for women in sports, and the broader impact of sport in fostering dialogue and solidarity across communities. These elements demonstrate significant positive social impact and meaningful progress in human rights and social development.
articles.personality_title: Sport unites communities as 2026 World Cup draws near
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – The 2026 World Cup will be held in Mexico, Canada, and the United States, bringing together 48 national football teams to compete in 104 games. This event arrives amid growing recognition of sport’s power to connect people and promote social change.

**What happened** – At the United Nations Headquarters in New York, athletes and sports leaders gathered on April 6 to discuss how football and other sports help break down barriers and build bridges between communities. They highlighted programs like Play Collective, which supports youth sports projects in host cities, and shared stories from inspiring figures like Nawal El Moutawakel, the first Muslim woman to win Olympic gold.

**Impact** – The meeting showed how sport goes beyond competition. It creates safe spaces for young people, encourages teamwork, and promotes gender equality. Nawal El Moutawakel’s Olympic success helped change attitudes about women in sports in Morocco and beyond. Programs like Play Collective reach millions of underserved youth, helping them find confidence and community.

**What’s next step** – As the World Cup kicks off in June, these initiatives will continue to grow, bringing more young people into sports and encouraging cooperation across borders. The 2028 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles will further highlight these efforts, promoting inclusion and equal opportunity in sports worldwide.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The upcoming World Cup and ongoing sports programs are powerful tools for connecting communities, empowering youth, and advancing social progress globally.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le sport unit les communautés à l'approche de la Coupe du Monde 2026
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – La Coupe du Monde 2026 se déroulera au Mexique, au Canada et aux États-Unis, réunissant 48 équipes nationales de football pour 104 matchs. Cet événement arrive alors que l'on reconnaît de plus en plus le pouvoir du sport pour connecter les personnes et promouvoir le changement social.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Au siège des Nations Unies à New York, des athlètes et des dirigeants sportifs se sont réunis le 6 avril pour discuter de la manière dont le football et d'autres sports aident à briser les barrières et à construire des ponts entre les communautés. Ils ont mis en avant des programmes comme Play Collective, qui soutient des projets sportifs pour les jeunes dans les villes hôtes, et ont partagé des histoires inspirantes, comme celle de Nawal El Moutawakel, première femme musulmane médaillée d'or olympique.

**Impact** – Cette rencontre a montré que le sport va au-delà de la compétition. Il crée des espaces sûrs pour les jeunes, encourage le travail d'équipe et promeut l'égalité des sexes. Le succès olympique de Nawal El Moutawakel a contribué à changer les mentalités sur les femmes dans le sport au Maroc et ailleurs. Des programmes comme Play Collective touchent des millions de jeunes défavorisés, les aidant à trouver confiance et communauté.

**Étape suivante** – À l'approche du coup d'envoi de la Coupe du Monde en juin, ces initiatives continueront à se développer, impliquant davantage de jeunes dans le sport et favorisant la coopération entre les pays. Les Jeux Olympiques d'été de 2028 à Los Angeles mettront encore plus en lumière ces efforts, promouvant l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances dans le sport à l'échelle mondiale.

**Résumé en une phrase** – La prochaine Coupe du Monde et les programmes sportifs en cours sont des outils puissants pour connecter les communautés, autonomiser les jeunes et faire avancer le progrès social dans le monde entier.
articles.personality_title_es: El deporte une comunidades ante la llegada del Mundial 2026
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – La Copa Mundial de 2026 se celebrará en México, Canadá y Estados Unidos, reuniendo a 48 equipos nacionales de fútbol para disputar 104 partidos. Este evento llega en un momento en que se reconoce cada vez más el poder del deporte para conectar a las personas y promover el cambio social.

**Qué pasó** – En la sede de las Naciones Unidas en Nueva York, atletas y líderes deportivos se reunieron el 6 de abril para hablar sobre cómo el fútbol y otros deportes ayudan a romper barreras y construir puentes entre comunidades. Destacaron programas como Play Collective, que apoya proyectos deportivos para jóvenes en las ciudades anfitrionas, y compartieron historias inspiradoras como la de Nawal El Moutawakel, la primera mujer musulmana en ganar una medalla de oro olímpica.

**Impacto** – La reunión mostró que el deporte va más allá de la competencia. Crea espacios seguros para los jóvenes, fomenta el trabajo en equipo y promueve la igualdad de género. El éxito olímpico de Nawal El Moutawakel ayudó a cambiar actitudes sobre las mujeres en el deporte en Marruecos y más allá. Programas como Play Collective llegan a millones de jóvenes desfavorecidos, ayudándoles a encontrar confianza y comunidad.

**Próximo paso** – Con el inicio de la Copa Mundial en junio, estas iniciativas continuarán creciendo, involucrando a más jóvenes en el deporte y fomentando la cooperación entre países. Los Juegos Olímpicos de Verano 2028 en Los Ángeles destacarán aún más estos esfuerzos, promoviendo la inclusión y la igualdad de oportunidades en el deporte a nivel mundial.

**Conclusión en una frase** – La próxima Copa Mundial y los programas deportivos en marcha son herramientas poderosas para conectar comunidades, empoderar a los jóvenes y avanzar en el progreso social a nivel global.
articles.image_prompt: A vibrant, detailed painting of a diverse group of silhouetted athletes running together on a stylized football pitch that blends seamlessly into a bridge spanning across three distinct landscapes representing Mexico, Canada, and the US, with abstract hurdles along the path symbolizing challenges overcome, all rendered in warm, earthy tones and soft natural colors to evoke unity, progress, and the transformative power of sport.

articles.title: Benefits and pensions rise as two-child cap ends
articles.summary: Families on some benefits with three or more children will get an average rise of £4,100 a year.
articles.content: "It's so draining. I'm exhausted worrying about money all the time. As a mum, sometimes you feel like you're failing, but I'm not failing, it's just the situation, unfortunately, that we are in," she said.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant policy change ending the two-child cap on benefits, resulting in an average rise of £4,100 a year for families with three or more children. This has a broad and meaningful positive impact on many families, improving their financial security and quality of life.
articles.personality_title: Families with three or more children to receive higher benefits after policy change
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – For several years, families receiving certain government benefits had a limit called the two-child cap. This rule meant they only got extra money for up to two children, even if they had more.

**What happened** – The government has now ended the two-child cap. This change means that families with three or more children will receive more money from benefits and pensions. On average, these families will get about £4,100 more each year.

**Impact** – This extra money can help families pay for things like food, clothes, and school supplies. Many parents have said that money worries made life very hard, so this change can reduce stress and improve their daily lives. It also means parents won’t feel like they are failing just because of financial struggles.

**What's next step** – The new policy will start being applied soon, and families affected will begin to see the extra money. Officials will likely watch how this change helps families and may consider other ways to support people in need.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Ending the two-child cap means larger families will get more financial support, easing money worries for many parents.
articles.personality_title_fr: Les familles de trois enfants ou plus bénéficient d'une augmentation des prestations après la fin du plafond à deux enfants
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Depuis plusieurs années, les familles recevant certaines prestations gouvernementales étaient limitées par un plafond appelé le « plafond à deux enfants ». Cette règle signifiait qu’elles ne recevaient de l’argent supplémentaire que pour deux enfants maximum, même si elles en avaient plus.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Le gouvernement a maintenant supprimé ce plafond à deux enfants. Ce changement signifie que les familles avec trois enfants ou plus recevront plus d’argent grâce aux prestations et pensions. En moyenne, ces familles recevront environ 4 100 £ de plus chaque année.

**Impact** – Cet argent supplémentaire peut aider les familles à payer des choses comme la nourriture, les vêtements et les fournitures scolaires. Beaucoup de parents ont dit que les soucis d’argent rendaient la vie très difficile, donc ce changement peut réduire le stress et améliorer leur quotidien. Cela signifie aussi que les parents ne se sentiront plus en échec à cause des difficultés financières.

**Prochaine étape** – La nouvelle politique sera bientôt mise en place, et les familles concernées commenceront à recevoir cet argent supplémentaire. Les responsables suivront sûrement comment ce changement aide les familles et pourraient envisager d’autres moyens de soutenir les personnes dans le besoin.

**Résumé en une phrase** – La fin du plafond à deux enfants permet aux familles nombreuses de recevoir plus d’aide financière, réduisant ainsi les soucis d’argent pour de nombreux parents.
articles.personality_title_es: Familias con tres o más hijos recibirán más beneficios tras eliminar el límite de dos niños
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Durante varios años, las familias que recibían ciertos beneficios del gobierno tenían un límite llamado el tope de dos hijos. Esta regla significaba que solo recibían dinero extra por hasta dos hijos, aunque tuvieran más.

**Qué pasó** – El gobierno ha eliminado este tope de dos hijos. Este cambio significa que las familias con tres o más hijos recibirán más dinero de beneficios y pensiones. En promedio, estas familias recibirán alrededor de £4,100 más al año.

**Impacto** – Este dinero extra puede ayudar a las familias a pagar cosas como comida, ropa y útiles escolares. Muchos padres han dicho que preocuparse por el dinero hacía la vida muy difícil, por lo que este cambio puede reducir el estrés y mejorar su vida diaria. También significa que los padres no se sentirán como si estuvieran fallando debido a problemas económicos.

**Próximo paso** – La nueva política comenzará a aplicarse pronto, y las familias afectadas empezarán a recibir el dinero extra. Los funcionarios probablemente observarán cómo esta medida ayuda a las familias y podrían considerar otras formas de apoyar a las personas necesitadas.

**Resumen en una frase** – El fin del tope de dos hijos significa que las familias numerosas recibirán más apoyo financiero, aliviando las preocupaciones económicas de muchos padres.
articles.image_prompt: A cozy, warmly lit home scene featuring two small, intertwined nests cradling three gently glowing eggs, symbolizing family growth and security, surrounded by softly textured blankets and a simple wooden table with a modest bowl of fruit, all rendered in gentle earth tones and soft brushstrokes to evoke comfort, hope, and renewed support.
